parameters.message: =тЬЕ Follow up upcoming appointments with PostgreSQL and Microsoft Teams on тАФ run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### Daily Schedule

Starts the workflow on a timer тАФ daily at 8:00. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Read Pending Queue (PostgreSQL)

### Read Pending Queue (PostgreSQL)

Runs a parameterized SQL statement against Postgres тАФ values are bound via `queryReplacement`, not string-interpolated, so it's immune to SQL injection.

Ports: тСатЖТ Stamp & Shape Booking ┬╖ тСбтЖТ Capture Failure Context

тЖ╗ retries 3├Ч/2s ┬╖ тЪа failures exit via the error rail ┬╖ ЁЯФС Postgres connection

### Stamp & Shape Booking

Shapes the item: writes `bookingId`, `receivedAt`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Payload Valid?

### Payload Valid?

Two-way gate on `={{ $json.bookingId }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: тСатЖТ Work in Batches ┬╖ тСбтЖТ Record Rejected Input

### Record Rejected Input

Runs a parameterized SQL statement against Postgres тАФ values are bound via `queryReplacement`, not string-interpolated, so it's immune to SQL injection.

тЖ╗ retries 3├Ч/2s ┬╖ ЁЯФС Postgres connection

### Work in Batches

Loop controller тАФ releases 20 item(s) per pass. Output 2 (loop) processes the batch, output 1 (done) fires after the last batch.

Ports: тСатЖТ Aggregate Batch Results ┬╖ тСбтЖТ Process Batch Item

### Process Batch Item

POST https://api.example.com/v1/bookings тАФ retries 3├Ч with backoff on failure.

Ports: тСатЖТ Respect Rate Limits ┬╖ тСбтЖТ Capture Failure Context

тЖ╗ retries 3├Ч/2s ┬╖ тЪа failures exit via the error rail ┬╖ ЁЯФС API key / bearer for the target API

### Respect Rate Limits

Pauses 2 seconds тАФ spacing calls to respect rate limits.

Next: Work in Batches

### Aggregate Batch Results

Collapses all items into a single summary item (for counts and run stats).

Next: Mark Completed in Google Sheets

### Mark Completed in Google Sheets

Upserts a row into 'Booking Tracker', matched on `bookingId` тАФ re-running with the same key updates the row instead of duplicating it.

Ports: тСатЖТ Save Execution Metadata ┬╖ тСбтЖТ Capture Failure Context

тЖ╗ retries 3├Ч/2s ┬╖ тЪа failures exit via the error rail ┬╖ ЁЯФС Google OAuth2 / service account

nodes.name: Note: Mark Completed in Google Sheets

parameters.content:
### Save Execution Metadata

Saves key fields onto the execution record тАФ searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Send Summary to Microsoft Teams

### Send Summary to Microsoft Teams

Posts a chat message into a Microsoft Teams channel тАФ same role as a Slack notify, for teams standardized on Teams.

тЖ╗ retries 3├Ч/2s ┬╖ тЪа failures exit via the error rail ┬╖ ЁЯФС Microsoft OAuth2

nodes.name: Note: Send Summary to Microsoft Teams

parameters.content:
### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Microsoft Teams

### Escalate Failure to Microsoft Teams

Posts a chat message into a Microsoft Teams channel тАФ same role as a Slack notify, for teams standardized on Teams.

тЖ╗ retries 3├Ч/2s ┬╖ ЁЯФС Microsoft OAuth2

nodes.name: Note: Escalate Failure to Microsoft Teams

parameters.content:
# Follow up upcoming appointments with PostgreSQL and Microsoft Teams on autopilot

**Pattern:** Reminders & Follow-ups ┬╖ **Level:** Core ┬╖ **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Follow up upcoming appointments with PostgreSQL and Microsoft Teams on autopilot. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual calendar sync is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Daily Schedule тЖТ Read Pending Queue (PostgreSQL) тЖТ Stamp & Shape Booking тЖТ Payload Valid? тЖТ Work in Batches тЖТ Aggregate Batch Results тЖТ Mark Completed in Google Sheets тЖТ тАж

## Use it for
- Teams that want to reminders & follow-ups without manual effort
- Anyone automating calendar sync in healthcare
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Improves response time
- Standardizes the process every time
- Responds in seconds

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; Google OAuth2 / service account; Microsoft OAuth2; Postgres connection.
Follow the color key: ЁЯЯж entry ┬╖ ЁЯЯк AI ┬╖ ЁЯЯл actions ┬╖ тмЬ utilities ┬╖ ЁЯЯй outputs ┬╖ ЁЯЯе error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath тАФ read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger тАФ hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click тЖТ Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

The batch size and the Wait between iterations exist specifically to respect the downstream API's rate limit тАФ processing everything as fast as possible just gets the workflow throttled or banned. Marking items completed only after a batch succeeds leaves a clean resume point if the run dies mid-way, instead of an ambiguous half-processed state.

## Practice exercises

Once it's wired up with real credentials, try:

1. Lower the batch size and raise the wait тАФ measure the throughput/reliability tradeoff directly.
2. Simulate a failed item mid-batch and see exactly what 'Mark Completed' captures.
3. Point the queue read at a different store platform.

## ЁЯЫЯ Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3├Ч with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail тЖТ context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## тП▒ Rate limiting

Work is batched and spaced with Wait so provider quotas aren't tripped. If you hit 429s, lower the batch size or raise the wait тАФ throughput drops, reliability rises.

## ЁЯЫа Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see ЁЯФС lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your calendar sync process. Every node card explains its role тАФ safe to extend at any marked branch.
